sources.name: Yoshi's Island Speedrunning Wiki — Fluttering
sources.notes: Flutter timing: 9 frames to maintain height, 6 frames to gain height, 1-frame release+repress for optimal flutter. Speed measurement: 808 sub-pixels/frame at ~3.156 px/frame.

sources.name: yoshi.science wiki — Movement
sources.notes: Navigation hub — references Frame Data and Disassembly wiki for detailed values.

sources.name: Yoshi's Island Disassembly — Raidenthequick / Arnethegreat
sources.notes: ROM-level disassembly reference. Specific values not extracted in current review.

notes: Yoshi's Island uses a SNES-native physics engine significantly different from Super Mario World.
Yoshi is the protagonist — Baby Mario rides on his back and acts as the hit detection system.
The flutter jump is the defining unique mechanic — mid-air flapping extends the jump.
Speed measurement from speedrunning community: 808 sub-pixels/frame ≈ 3.156 px/f (run speed estimate).
Flutter timing: 9-frame window to maintain height, 6-frame window to gain height.
Egg throw is 8-directional aiming with a targeting cursor.
Ground pound (press down in air) allows attacking from above and activating certain blocks.

subpixel_system.notes: Sub-pixel system confirmed by speedrunning community (808 sub-px/frame measurement). Exact sub-pixels-per-pixel unit needs ROM verification.

walk_speed.notes: Approximate. Yoshi is more agile than Mario. VERIFY.

run_speed.source: Speedrunning community: 'Gets 808 subpixels per frame while traveling at full run speed'
run_speed.notes: If sub-pixels-per-pixel = 256: 808/256 = 3.156 px/f. If 320: 808/320 = 2.525 px/f. VERIFY sub-pixel unit.

initial_y_velocity_approx.notes: Approximate from gameplay observation. VERIFY against ROM.

jump.notes: Standard variable height jump before flutter kicks in.

flutter_jump._note: THE DEFINING MECHANIC. The flutter extends jump height significantly and is the primary Yoshi traversal skill.
flutter_jump.activation: Hold jump button after the initial jump reaches apex — Yoshi starts fluttering.

optimal_reflutter.description: Release jump and repress within 1 frame at the right moment to restart the flutter at maximum efficiency.

max_height_technique.description: Release B 8 frames before the float sprite appears for 1 frame, then hold.

notes: Flutter provides meaningful additional height — enough to reach platforms just out of range of the standard jump.
Experienced players chain flutter timing optimally (1-frame repress technique) for maximum height.
The flutter animation shows Yoshi flapping rapidly — the visual feedback matches the mechanical timing window.
Flutter does not cancel horizontal momentum — Yoshi continues moving horizontally while fluttering.

carrying.notes: Yoshi can carry up to 6 eggs. Eggs are acquired by swallowing certain enemies.

aiming.description: Hold Y to aim with a crosshair cursor. Press R to throw in cursor direction.
notes: The aiming cursor can be positioned anywhere within range.
8-directional targeting allows hitting enemies above, below, and diagonally.
Eggs bounce off walls and terrain, allowing indirect hits.
Different egg types (normal, watermelon, fire, ice) have different behaviors.

trajectory.notes: Eggs arc downward after throwing — like a real thrown projectile with gravity.

ground_pound.description: Yoshi slams downward, crashing into the ground with impact force.
notes: Ground pound damages enemies directly below on contact.
Activates certain level triggers and flip panels.
Can break certain floor types.

baby_mario_system._note: Baby Mario is on Yoshi's back. His presence or absence is the health system.
baby_mario_system.normal_state: Baby Mario rides on Yoshi's back

hit_state.description: When Yoshi takes damage, Baby Mario falls off and floats in a bubble, crying. The Star Counter begins counting down.

star_counter.description: Upon losing Baby Mario, stars begin counting down from the current count (up to 30). Must catch Baby Mario before stars reach 0 to rescue him.
star_counter.rescue_mechanic: Jump into the bubble to recatch Baby Mario
star_counter.fail_condition: Stars hit 0 = stage fail (Baby Mario is gone)

notes: This system is unique in platformer design — losing a life isn't instant. Players have a countdown to recover.
The star count creates tension proportional to how exposed you've left Baby Mario floating in a bubble.
Getting hit repeatedly (losing stars from being hit while already in danger) creates compounding stress.
Stars can be collected to build up the countdown buffer (up to 30 stars max).

tongue.description: Yoshi can extend his tongue to grab enemies and items from range.
tongue.notes: Swallowing an enemy converts it to an egg. Some enemies cannot be swallowed.

yoshi.notes: Yoshi's hitbox is his body — approximately 16×16 pixels. VERIFY.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: The Baby Mario bubble/star-counter damage system is a design innovation that makes 'getting hit' interesting rather than just punishing. The countdown period after losing Mario creates active recovery gameplay — the miss doesn't end the run, it creates urgency.

The flutter jump's 1-frame optimal timing creates a skill ceiling that reveals itself gradually. Casual players use the flutter as a safety net; expert players time it precisely for maximum reach. Same mechanic, two different mastery levels.

The egg targeting cursor is the most 'aim-and-fire' mechanic in the documented set. Unlike most platformers where projectiles go straight, Yoshi's eggs require aim selection before throwing. This slows combat down intentionally — contemplative rather than reactive.

Yoshi's Island's visual aesthetic (crayon/paint texture) combined with the infant-and-baby-on-back narrative create a unique emotional register. The level design uses this — levels feel like they're drawn by a child who imagined a fantastic world.
The ground pound mechanic pre-dates its use in later Mario games (SM64's stomp was influenced by this). YI's ground pound is combat + puzzle tool: stomp enemies, trigger switches, break floors.
